title: user-intelligence: add stripe smurf-watch panel + clarify billing-address country
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds Stripe forensics to the `user-intelligence` Grafana dashboard. Investigating where our Stripe revenue actually comes from surfaced Macao reported as the #1 country by billing address — but those charges are nearly all US-issued cards. There was no panel in the dashboard exposing this disagreement, and no equivalent of "Risky Users" for successful payments.

Three new panels + one enhancement on `user-intelligence.json`:

- **Top Buyers — Smurf Watch** (new) — successful-payment counterpart to "Risky Users". Surfaces emails with 5+ paid checkouts, `distinct_stripe_customers` (number of separate Stripe Customer objects the email generated), `smurf_ratio`, and `distinct_billing_countries`. Joined to `d1_user` for tier and ban. Enriched in the second commit with `card_countries`, `card_brands`, and a `billing_vs_card` match/mismatch pill (LEFT JOIN to `charge.succeeded` by payment-intent ID).

- **Top Countries by Card Issuer (Verified)** (new) — bar chart at the bottom under a new "Card Issuer Geography" section, revenue grouped by the country that issued the card. Direct comparison target for the existing billing-address chart, which is renamed to make the self-reported limitation explicit.

- **Billing × Card Country Mismatch** (new) — table of successful charges where billing ≠ card. Already shows `MO→US/amex 11 charges`, `ZA→US/amex 5 charges` in the first 2 days of data.

- **Top Countries by Revenue** (existing, renamed) — now "(Billing Address — self-reported)" with an expanded description pointing at the carding pattern surfaced by the failures panels and the new mismatch panel.

Card data flows from PR #11158 (charge.succeeded webhook + 10 new columns on `stripe_event`); only events after that deploy will have card columns populated, no backfill. Within a week of normal traffic the empties become the minority.

Validated all SQL against live Tinybird. Deployed for preview on the droplet from this branch; once merged, re-deploy from `origin/main` with the

gist.headline: Following the digital paper trail
gist.blurb: Added new Stripe forensics panels to our internal dashboards to track payment patterns and card-issuer mismatches. Keeping the hive safe from sneaky smurfs!
gist.summary: Added Stripe forensics panels to the user-intelligence Grafana dashboard, including a 'Smurf Watch' panel and tracking for card-issuer versus billing-address mismatches.
gist.impact: Internal teams can now monitor suspicious payment behaviors, such as emails generating multiple Stripe customer objects or mismatches between self-reported billing addresses and actual card issuer countries.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee as a detective analyzing data. Key symbols: magnifying glass, retro monitor with charts, detective hat.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny detective hat and holding a magnifying glass, inspecting a retro computer monitor displaying colorful bar charts and data tables. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
